# MDS 6-Hour War Room Command Centre v0

Status: active docs-only command centre
Created: 2026-06-18
Root: `D:/Million Dollar AI Studio/`

## STATUS

MDS now has a Board-aware CEO/runtime governance spine, an active Master Operating Index, v7.1 canonical strategy, Harness Engineering propagation, and a VCOS Roster routing layer for department/tool/gate dispatch. The current work is to make the company executable by routing every meaningful request through one accountable owner, one tool path, one gate, one evidence contract, and one closeout loop.

MRR remains `$0` unless verified payment evidence proves otherwise.

Latest authority update: Shrish selected the D-PROD operating model on 2026-06-26. `D:/Million Dollar AI Studio/` is the active MDS operating PROD root for doctrine, state, VCOS, Command Centre, local repos/worktrees, local verification artifacts, and tooling. GitHub is authoritative for committed/versioned code and deployment source commits. Provider dashboards are authoritative for live provider-owned runtime, deployment, database, schema, billing, payment, auth, and environment state. `E:/Million Dollar AI Studio/` is backup/recovery only. Google Drive is optional non-authoritative storage/reference only. Session extracts under D are evidence inputs until verified and promoted.

Latest update: Operator Access Readiness bootstrap is complete as a D-prod control-plane pack. Codex tested the available routes for Claude desktop/Cowork, Claude Code CLI, browser automation, Antigravity, Git/GitHub, Vercel, Railway, Supabase, Stripe, Docker, Ollama, Node/npm, and provider project linkage without reading secrets. Verdict: access is partial-ready. Ollama API, Antigravity CLI, Git local checks, Node/npm, and D-prod file-backed handoffs are usable. Claude Code CLI is installed but not logged in; GitHub CLI token is invalid; Vercel/Railway/Supabase CLIs are not on PATH; Docker daemon is unavailable; browser/desktop automation helper is blocked with `CreateProcessAsUserW failed: 5`; Stripe works only through a D-local unauthenticated config route. New rule: Shrish performs local logins, Codex/CEO run no-secret status verification, and state records READY / PARTIAL / BLOCKED / UNKNOWN without secret values.

## Source Doctrine

| Source | Status | Authority |
|---|---|---|
| `D:/Million Dollar AI Studio/` | Active operating PROD root | MDS internal doctrine, state, VCOS, Command Centre, Master Operating Index, local execution, local verification artifacts |
| GitHub | Code authority | Committed/versioned code, branches, tags, releases, deployment source commits |
| Provider dashboards | Live-state authority | Runtime, deployment, database, schema, billing, payment, auth, environment, provider-owned state |
| `E:/Million Dollar AI Studio/` | Backup/recovery only | Not active source |
| Google Drive | Optional non-authoritative storage/reference | Not boot source, code workspace, deployment source, or canonical memory |
| Session extracts under D | Evidence inputs | Not doctrine until verified and promoted |
| `F:/Million Dollar AI Studio/` | Historical/reference unless reconciled | Not active source |

## Current Operating Stack

| CEO kernel | active | `CEO.md` |

| Gwalia/FoodOps | parked client-service candidate | PARKED_BY_SHRISH_DIRECTIVE / NO_SEND | UNKNOWN | Exact no-send message and call-prep packet exists, but Gwalia is no longer an active lane | Do not continue unless Shrish explicitly reopens Gwalia |

## Five-Product Demo Mode

| Product | Demo readiness | Demo pack | Next demo action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Astro AI Studio | PARTIAL / INTERNAL_DEMO_READY | `product-demo/astro-consumer-pro-internal-demo-walkthrough.md` | Completed; authenticated Pro/provider proof remains gated |
| Prep AI Studio | DEMO_READY | `product-demo/prep-no-provider-internal-demo-walkthrough.md` | Completed; provider status checklist remains human gate |
| Pathway AI Studio | PARTIAL | `product-demo/pathway-regulated-safe-internal-demo-walkthrough.md` | Completed; legal/provider proof remains gated |
| ATLAS | PARTIAL / SAFETY_GATED | `product-demo/atlas-demo-pack.md` | Security/Compliance review and provider verification |
| NestIQ | PARTIAL | `product-demo/nestiq-demo-pack.md` | Frontend route inventory and housing/legal do-not-claim review |

## Active Lanes

| Lane | Status | Next safe action |
|---|---|---|
| CEO / Board / tooling | active / access bootstrap required | Complete Claude Code and GitHub CLI login first, then rerun no-secret access verifier |
| Frontend completion sprint | active safe patches complete | Prep local Node 20 proof passed with warnings; GitHub hosted runner remains blocked by billing/payment gate |
| Product readiness | active | Preserve frontend-first; keep backend/payment/auth/database as blockers or stubs |
| Payment/booking readiness | blocked/evidence needed | Keep manual invoice/payment path approval-gated; no live payment action |
| Security readiness | audit complete; Prep docs cleanup minimal review packet ready | Review the packet; runtime payment changes stay gated |
| Workflow Diagnostic revenue sprint | parked by Shrish directive | Do not continue prospect/outreach work unless explicitly reopened |
| Gwalia/FoodOps | parked by Shrish directive | Do not continue Gwalia work unless explicitly reopened |
| Content/marketing | active content moat / draft-only | Route Content Marketing after VCOS Roster validation; first exact-copy approval batch comes from `content/review-queue/2026-06-20-mds-content-moat-open-source-harness-command-centre-post-pack.md` |
| VCOS automation factory | specs only | Keep read-only/draft generators first |
| Open-source / Skill-MCP Inspector | spec only | No public release without approval |
| Master Operating Index | active memory map | Update after meaningful changes |

## Approval Strip

Hard-gated actions requiring Shrish approval:

- Destructive or irreversible deploy/migration actions.
- Database deletion/drop/reset/truncate/overwrite actions.
- Pricing changes.
- Payment/refund/checkout/invoice/payment-link/customer/subscription/charge actions.
- External messages and client commitments.
- File deletion.
- Secret, credential, cloud-permission, or production-provider changes.
- Regulated-domain claims.
- Public open-source release.
- Live/revenue/payment/deployment claims without evidence.

Scoped deploy/auth/schema/migration engineering may proceed when it has a work order, target environment, evidence, stop/rollback plan, and closeout. Payment object creation and secret handling remain hard-gated.

## Top Blockers

1. Product live/revenue/payment/deployment/schema states remain `UNKNOWN` unless evidence exists.
2. Prep provider states remain `UNKNOWN`; CLI route captured partial Railway/Stripe metadata but Vercel/Supabase CLI checks are blocked, Railway is unlinked, and manual dashboard verification is now the shortest path.
3. Prep dirty WIP touches payment/auth/migrations/platform areas and remains quarantined from the verification branch.
4. Astro payment proof requires Shrish dashboard/card verification and approval gates.
5. Gwalia/FoodOps is parked by Shrish directive; client contact remains blocked.
6. Prep Stripe/Razorpay retirement plan is complete; Phase 1 docs-only Stripe cleanup has a minimal four-file review packet. Launch/deploy remains blocked by runtime payment ambiguity.
7. Workflow Diagnostic pricing/send packet and 3-prospect worksheet are complete, but outreach/prospect-selection work is now parked by Shrish directive.

## NEXT ACTION

Review and validate the Codex Strategic Board v2 draft before installation.

## QUEUE

1. Preserve historical control-plane audit v1 as immutable evidence.
2. Run authority reconciliation verifier after Board v2 draft review.
3. Back up currently installed Codex custom instructions before any install attempt.
4. Shrish approves exact Board v2 custom-instruction text before installation.
5. Return to product/revenue execution with verified provider/GitHub evidence instead of stale assumptions.

## PARKED

Gwalia, Workflow Diagnostic outreach, full Autopilot, NEXUS, Board polish, Mentor v2, broad strategy rewrites, public open-source release, external posting, destructive deploy/migration actions, and payment-object actions are parked until the relevant gates are clear.

# Product Readiness Board

Status: active docs-only board
Created: 2026-06-18

## Classification Key

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| READY | Enough evidence exists to route the next non-red action. |
| BLOCKED | Progress requires approval, missing evidence, or risk decision. |
| UNKNOWN | Evidence is missing or conflicting. |
| NEEDS FIX | Active lane has known gaps that can be audited/planned safely. |
| PARKED | Not active execution; passive R&D or out of current 6-hour scope. |

## Board

# Product VCOS Instance Board

Status: active Command Centre board
Owner: VCOS Architect/Builder Studio / Product Ops Studio
Created: 2026-07-01

## Purpose

This board proves the canonical Product VCOS template has five local instances and gives the Command Centre a structured source for Product VCOS status.

Source CSV:

- `command-centre/war-room/product-vcos-instance-board.csv`

Template:

- `vcos/_shared/product-vcos/product-vcos-template.md`
- `vcos/_shared/product-vcos/product-vcos-template.yaml`

## Instances

| Product | Instance | Status | Current next action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Astro AI Studio / Vedic Astro Studio | `vcos/products/astro-ai-studio/product-vcos.yaml` | active instance with provider/payment/schema/revenue unknowns | Capture authenticated Pro visual proof after Node LTS/CI/container route exists. |
| Prep AI Studio | `vcos/products/prep-ai-studio/product-vcos.yaml` | active instance with provider states unknown | Complete manual provider dashboard checklist with READY / MISSING / UNKNOWN statuses only. |
| Pathway AI Studio | `vcos/products/pathway-ai-studio/product-vcos.yaml` | active prelaunch regulated-domain instance | Use shared Node LTS / Docker / CI proof route before rerunning authenticated proof. |
| NestIQ | `vcos/products/nestiq/product-vcos.yaml` | passive R&D instance | Run clean frontend proof if reactivated. |
| ATLAS | `vcos/products/atlas/product-vcos.yaml` | source-confirmed safety-gated instance | Complete Security/Compliance review and provider verification before any launch. |

## Required Capabilities Covered

Each instance includes product memory, release management, backlog, user feedback, telemetry, support, QA, security, AI cost monitoring, revenue, churn, upsell triggers, content, marketing, weekly review, knowledge promotion, and NEXUS candidate extraction.

## Command Centre Rule

The local Command Centre may display these Product VCOS rows as D-local operating state. It must not convert them into live provider, payment, revenue, customer, churn, telemetry, deployment, auth, schema, or public-claim proof.

## Next Action

VCOS Architect/Builder Studio reviews the first weekly Product VCOS run after one product moves and decides whether the template needs a verifier, schema hardening, or Command Centre UI expansion.

Fresh private paired Terminal-Bench fast5 rerun now shows raw Ollama `qwen3-coder:30b` 2/5 versus MIDAS-harnessed `qwen3-coder:30b` 5/5 with item-level evidence and hashes. Additional private evidence includes one SWE-bench Lite MIDAS prediction resolved through manual official-instance replay and one Qwen Coder LiveCodeBench micro lift from raw 2/3 to MIDAS 3/3. These are private engineering results only, not official public leaderboard scores and not claims that MIDAS beats Claude/GPT/Gemini or all frameworks. Next run a larger paired Terminal-Bench subset with the same evidence packet shape and unblock replayable Claude/GPT/Gemini comparator routes; no public release/push/npm publish/public claim without approval. |

protectedInvariants.statement: Secret values, credentials, tokens, private keys, auth stores, and environment files are never read, printed, summarized, or committed by default.
Public framework files must stay source-neutral and must not include non-public operating material, product-specific context, provenance trails, or unsupported benchmark claims.
Completion, release readiness, benchmark, security, and compatibility claims require local evidence recorded in the run ledger or a reviewable artifact.
Deploys, public releases, public claims, money movement, destructive operations, external messages, and approval-policy changes require explicit approval before execution.

escalationRules.trigger: Deleting, overwriting, rewriting history, or performing a hard-to-reverse local operation.
Reading, printing, forwarding, storing, or modifying secrets, credentials, tokens, private keys, environment files, or auth/browser storage.
Changing provider, auth, model route, endpoint, account, webhook, or external-service configuration.
Publishing, pushing, packaging for public release, submitting a benchmark, or making a public performance/security/compatibility claim.
Creating or changing prices, checkouts, invoices, subscriptions, refunds, charges, or other money-moving records.
Deploying, migrating data, changing production schemas, or modifying live infrastructure.

profiles.description: Analyze scope, risks, and implementation sequence before changes.
Implement scoped work orders with approval-aware edits and verification.
Review code, docs, and release evidence without making changes.
Gather bounded external or dependency context without workspace mutation.

profiles.name: Focused Run
profiles.purpose: Keeps a single scoped work order bounded to local project files, explicit evidence, and a short feedback loop.

profiles.name: Long-Horizon Run
profiles.purpose: Controls multi-step work that may use delegated subtasks while preserving checkpoints, inherited approvals, and closeout evidence.

profiles.name: External Action Review
profiles.purpose: Prepares approval packets for external, release, or public-facing actions without performing those actions by default.

gateways.name: Local Operator Gateway
gateways.purpose: A local control-plane contract for maintainer-driven project work with explicit schema checks and run-ledger evidence.

gateways.name: Private Control Gateway
gateways.purpose: An authenticated private-network control plane for supervised operator sessions, streamed events, and bounded tool access.

gateways.name: Reviewed Automation Gateway
gateways.purpose: An external automation ingress contract for scheduled or webhook-driven runs that require approved origins and human-reviewed high-risk actions.

packs.name: Project Docs
packs.purpose: Keeps project documentation available as a reviewed, source-cited knowledge pack for planning and implementation work.

sourcePolicy.sourceRetention: Keep source references and remove stale or disallowed entries during workspace review.

retrievalTests.query: What is this project trying to accomplish?
retrievalTests.expectedEvidence: Answer must cite the project context or a reviewed project document.

packs.feedbackLoop: Incorrect or stale answers should create a reviewed work order before the pack is refreshed.

packs.name: Release Evidence
packs.purpose: Groups release notes, validation summaries, and run evidence so closeouts can be checked against reviewed proof.

sourcePolicy.sourceRetention: Keep evidence paths and remove entries that contain private, secret, or unreviewed source material.

retrievalTests.query: What evidence proves the latest completed run?
retrievalTests.expectedEvidence: Answer must cite a run ledger entry, release note, or validation summary.

packs.feedbackLoop: Missing or contradictory evidence should reopen the run closeout before the pack is refreshed.

packs.name: Support Knowledge
packs.purpose: Defines a safe intake shape for reviewed support answers, issue notes, and user-provided material without secret or PII storage.

sourcePolicy.sourceRetention: Keep only reviewed non-sensitive support context and remove user-provided material on request.

retrievalTests.query: Which reviewed source supports this support answer?
retrievalTests.expectedEvidence: Answer must cite a reviewed support note, issue, or public documentation source.

packs.feedbackLoop: Support corrections should be reviewed, cited, and re-tested before becoming reusable knowledge.

components.purpose: Validate reusable instruction packs before they are trusted by a project or shared outside the workspace.

dimensions.evidence: Skill descriptions must include trigger-ready language so activation is intentional.
Default skill packs stay narrow and subordinate to work orders, source files, and approval gates.
Skill bodies are kept under the framework line budget and can move detail into references when needed.
Allowed-tools hints are validated and dangerous pre-approval patterns are rejected.
Verification-oriented skill guidance requires evidence before completion claims.
Suspicious body language that weakens approval, review, or verification is advisory-visible.
Installed skill packs are validated during workspace and repository checks.

risks: Semantic unsafe instructions can still require human review.
A passing score does not prove prompt-injection resistance.

releaseGate.evidenceRequired: Run midas skills, midas quality, and midas validate before release review.

components.purpose: Check generated harness guidance for portability, source-of-truth posture, and drift before operators rely on it.

dimensions.evidence: Adapter guidance begins with explicit load order and active modules.
Generated adapters are framed as project harness guidance, not autonomous authority.
Adapter files have size budgets and shared policy rendering instead of duplicating long manuals.
Supported harness outputs have adapter contracts and inventory hashes.
Adapters render shared evidence requirements and workflow closeout expectations.
Adapters render shared approval gates and protected-content rules.
Harness inventory and context snapshots detect missing or changed adapter artifacts.

risks: Generated adapter guidance still needs runtime smoke testing in each external harness.
Harness-specific capability changes can make contracts stale.

releaseGate.evidenceRequired: Run midas adapters, midas inventory, midas context, and midas validate before release review.

components.purpose: Keep public readiness, benchmark, and distribution claims tied to deterministic evidence instead of narrative confidence.

dimensions.evidence: Claim-bearing records name the specific component and distribution surface being reviewed.
Claim levels are bounded to local-validation unless explicit release review raises them.
The scorecard points to other MIDAS evidence catalogs rather than embedding logs.
Release review includes adapter, skill, and workspace evidence rather than assuming one harness.
Benchmark and public-release claims require receipts, hashes, and approval state elsewhere in MIDAS.
Public release readiness requires approval even when local scores pass.
The scorecard itself is installed, inspected, captured in context, and validated.

risks: Scores are deterministic readiness signals, not quality badges or public certification.
Public release claims remain blocked until the full release gate passes.

releaseGate.evidenceRequired: Run midas quality, midas benchmarks, public leak scan, package smoke, and release review before publication.
